rationale: The ensures is `<antecedent> ==> det_equal`, so the antecedent is a hypothesis only inside an `if <antecedent> { ... }` branch — the prior attempt asserted antecedent components (e.g. `post1_netc.my_end_point() == pre_netc.my_end_point()`) at the top of the body and Verus correctly rejected them. Wrapping the assertions inside the antecedent-true branch surfaces the per-run facts with tuple-projection triggers (instead of the shadowed `let` names), restates the `ios == map_sent_packet_seq_to_ios(packets@)` pin and the `raw_io_consistent_with_spec_io` unfolding for both runs, and closes the joint-success case via sequence extensionality (`=~=`).
